stories.title: D49 Budget Vote Is Tomorrow — What COS Families Should Know
stories.angle: District 49's Board of Education votes tomorrow (June 11) at 6:30 PM at Sand Creek High School (7005 Carefree Cir N) on the 2026-27 budget. The district declared fiscal exigency in January 2026 and in February approved nearly $2 million in program and staff cuts, including reductions in non-core teacher positions, supplemental staffing, and administrative roles. Programs like Base49, PEAK, preschool, and Elevates 18-21 were listed as impacted. The budget is open for public inspection at the CFO's office. COS families who attend D49 schools should know this vote finalizes cuts that take effect July 1.

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
D49 families — budget vote is TOMORROW.

June 11, 6:30 PM, Sand Creek High School.

Nearly $2M in cuts are on the table. Public welcome. Details at d49.org.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

research.facts:
D49 Budget Vote Is Tomorrow — What COS Families Should Know
**Decision:** FOLLOW-UP — D49 budget vote June 11 was covered June 8 (preview angle). Today's angle: vote is tomorrow, final reminder, what's at stake.
**Sources:**
- d49.org — Board of Education meeting page, budget filing notice (June 2026)
- koaa.com — "'We are stripping the district down to absolute essentials,' School District 49 grapples with budget crisis" (2026)
- gazette.com — "D-49 board approves staff, program cuts to balance budget" (February 12, 2026)
- pikespeakbulletin.org — "District 49 to cut programs, teachers amid 'fiscal exigency'" (2026)
**Key Facts:**
- Vote: June 11, 2026 at 6:30 PM, Sand Creek High School, 7005 Carefree Cir N, Colorado Springs CO
- Fiscal exigency declared: January 2026 (board declared financial emergency)
- February 2026: Board approved nearly $2 million in cuts (4-1 vote)
- Cuts include: administrative staffing reductions, supplemental program reductions, vendor contract renegotiations, supplemental staffing reductions, teacher reductions in non-core subjects
- Programs impacted: Base49, PEAK, preschool, Elevates 18-21 (per June 9 pipeline data)
- Enrollment decline: projected loss ~$400,000 at current per-pupil rate
- State per-pupil funding: state proposed budget includes increase
- Budget open for public inspection at CFO's office
**Freshness:** Vote is TOMORROW (June 11) — maximum timeliness. This is the correct day for a reminder post.
**Confidence:** HIGH (meeting date/location confirmed from d49.org; budget cuts from gazette.com/KOAA confirmed independently)

stories.title: Guidecraft Kitchen Helper Tower Recall — 25,000+ Units, Fall Hazard
stories.angle: CPSC recalled approximately 25,235 Guidecraft Classic and Contemporary Kitchen Helper Towers on May 14, 2026. The platform inside can loosen over time, become unstable, and detach — posing a serious fall hazard to toddlers. There have been 11 reported incidents, including 3 injuries (bloody nose, bruises). The towers were sold at Amazon, Wayfair, Walmart, Target, Guidecraft.com, and other retailers from June 2022 through October 2023 for about $200. Remedy: free repair parts. Stop using immediately. Contact Guidecraft at 800-524-3555 (Mon–Fri, 8 AM–8 PM ET) or visit CPSC.gov for details.

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
RECALL: Guidecraft Kitchen Helper Towers — 25,235 units.

Platform can loosen and detach over time — fall hazard for toddlers.

Sold 2022-2023 at Amazon, Walmart, Target (~$200). Stop using. Free repair: 800-524-3555.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
Guidecraft Kitchen Helper Tower recall: check the foil sticker on the bottom for your item and lot numbers.

11 reports of children falling. 3 injuries. Free repair kit from Guidecraft: 800-524-3555.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

research.facts:
Guidecraft Kitchen Helper Tower Recall — 25,000+ Units, Fall Hazard
**Decision:** NEW STORY — Guidecraft Kitchen Helper Tower recall (announced May 14, 2026). Not covered in previous pipeline runs.
**Sources:**
- CPSC.gov — "Guidecraft Children's Standing Towers Recalled Due to Risk of Serious Injury from Fall Hazard" (May 14, 2026, Recall #26490)
- dailyhornet.com — "Guidecraft Recalls 25,000 Toddler Towers for Fall Hazard" (2026)
- schiffmanfirm.com — Guidecraft Children's Standing Towers Recalled (recall summary)
**Key Facts:**
- Units recalled: approximately 25,235
- Products: Classic and Contemporary Guidecraft Kitchen Helper Towers (9 colors)
- Sale period: June 1, 2022 through October 31, 2023
- Retailers: Amazon, Wayfair, Walmart, Target, Guidecraft.com, Maisonette, Overstock
- Price: approximately $200
- Hazard: platform can loosen over time → becomes unstable/detaches → fall hazard for toddlers
- Incidents: 11 reports of children falling
- Injuries: 3 (bloody nose, bruises — none severe)
- How to identify: check item number and lot number on foil sticker at bottom of platform (specific numbers listed on CPSC.gov)
- Remedy: FREE repair parts (contact Guidecraft for parts + installation instructions)
- Contact: 800-524-3555, Mon–Fri 8 AM–8 PM ET
- More info: CPSC.gov (Recall #26490)
**Freshness:** Recall announced May 14, 2026 — within the 30-day priority window for safety recalls; families may not have seen this yet.
**Confidence:** HIGH (CPSC.gov is primary source; unit count/injury count from CPSC official page, confirmed by 2 secondary sources)

stories.title: Free Family Day at Western Museum of Mining & Industry — This Saturday June 12
stories.angle: The Western Museum of Mining and Industry hosts a free Family Day this Saturday, June 12. Families can watch the stamp mill machinery in action, see a live blacksmith demonstration, take a tractor-pulled hayride, and watch the Gold Panning Championships. All outdoor activities are free. The museum is at 225 North Gate Blvd, Colorado Springs CO 80921. Phone: 719-488-0880. Great option for families looking for a low-cost local outing before the Renaissance Festival opens next weekend.

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Free COS family outing this Saturday.

June 12: Family Day at the Western Museum of Mining & Industry.

Stamp mill, blacksmith demo, hayride, Gold Panning — all free outdoors.

225 North Gate Blvd. wmmi.org

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

stories.title: Father's Day Weekend Guide — COS Families' Best Options (June 21)
stories.angle: Father's Day is June 21 — 11 days away. COS families have great local options: Pikes Peak offers free admission for dads on June 20 (note: closed June 21 for Pikes Peak Hill Climb); Colorado Wolf and Wildlife Center has Family Day June 21, 9–11 AM (reservations required); Garden of the Gods is always free; Flying W Ranch gives dads a free hat. The Colorado Renaissance Festival (opened June 13-14 in Larkspur) runs Saturdays and Sundays, making the June 21 Renaissance Festival visit a natural Father's Day option. Pikes Peak Hill Climb also on June 21 — a unique family event. Planning ahead helps avoid the day-of scramble.

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
Father's Day tip for COS families:

Pikes Peak free for dads is June 20 — NOT June 21 (closed for Hill Climb).

Also June 21: Renaissance Festival (Larkspur), Garden of the Gods (free), Wolf Center.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

research.facts:
Father's Day Weekend Guide — COS Families' Best Options (June 21)
**Decision:** NEW STORY — Father's Day is June 21, 2026. No previous pipeline coverage of Father's Day planning guide.
**Sources:**
- pikes-peak.com — "Father's Day Activities in Colorado Springs" 2026
- visitcos.com — Annual Events 2026, Father's Day section
- cospringsmom.com — "Nine Local Father's Day Activities"
- coloradorenfaire.com — Colorado Renaissance Festival schedule (runs Sat/Sun through Aug 2, including June 21)
**Key Facts:**
- Father's Day: June 21, 2026
- Pikes Peak: Free admission for dads June 20 (Note: closed June 21 for Pikes Peak International Hill Climb race)
- Pikes Peak International Hill Climb: June 21 — 12.42-mile course, 156 turns, 9,390 ft to 14,115 ft summit; family spectator event
- Colorado Wolf and Wildlife Center: June 21, 9–11 AM; reservations required (call ahead)
- Garden of the Gods: always free; picnic and hiking
- Flying W Ranch: dads get a free hat on Father's Day
- Colorado Renaissance Festival: runs Sat/Sun through Aug 2 — June 21 is a Sunday show day in Larkspur (30 min from COS)
- Note: if planning Pikes Peak drive with dad, must go June 20 (free) not June 21 (closed for Hill Climb)
- Miramont Castle: Cigars & Bourbon in the Gardens June 16, $55/person (adults without kids)
**Freshness:** Father's Day is 11 days away — timely planning guide.
**Confidence:** MEDIUM (event details from multiple sources; some details need call-ahead verification for specific hours)

stories.title: D11 Orton Academy Moving to Trailblazer Elementary for 2026-27
stories.angle: Orton Academy — a D11 charter school specializing in teaching students with dyslexia, opened in 2020 — will move into Trailblazer Elementary School for the 2026-27 school year. The move offers a shared campus model for a specialized program that serves K-8 students with dyslexia. For D11 families currently enrolled in or considering Orton Academy, this is a location and campus change to plan around. Orton Academy's approach is research-based (Orton-Gillingham method) and it's one of very few K-8 dyslexia-focused schools in Colorado Springs.

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
D11 has a free public school for kids with dyslexia.

Orton Academy (K-8) uses the Orton-Gillingham method. It's moving to Trailblazer Elementary for 2026-27.

1 in 5 kids has dyslexia. Enroll at d11.org.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

research.facts:
D11 Orton Academy Moving to Trailblazer Elementary for 2026-27
**Decision:** NEW STORY — Orton Academy relocation announcement. Not covered in previous pipeline runs.
**Sources:**
- gazette.com — "Colorado Springs District 11 charter school to move in with elementary school" (April 8, 2026)
- d11.org — School portfolio / choice enrollment page
**Key Facts:**
- School: Orton Academy — D11 charter school, K-8
- Specialty: Teaches students with dyslexia using Orton-Gillingham research-based method
- Founded: 2020
- Moving: to Trailblazer Elementary School for the 2026-27 school year (shared campus model)
- Why it matters: Location change for currently enrolled families; new option for families discovering it
- Orton-Gillingham: multisensory, structured literacy approach; recognized by IDA (International Dyslexia Association)
- Context: ~1 in 5 children has some form of dyslexia; most D11 families may not know this specialized school exists
- Enrollment info: d11.org choice enrollment portal
- Contact for families: d11.org
**Freshness:** April 2026 announcement — still relevant for fall 2026 planning (enrollment decisions happening now for 2026-27).
**Confidence:** MEDIUM (single primary source: gazette.com; d11.org choice enrollment confirms Orton Academy as a D11 charter option)
